summary: Matched the common fighter cargo jump action by replacing the corresponding ft_08A4 assembly with a new C implementation and header, adding the new object to the build, and updating dependent fighter/common source files to include the corrected ft_08A4 declarations. The PR report shows `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CargoJump.c` at 100% for the file and for `ftCo_8009BB1C`, `ftCo_8009BB64`, `ftCo_CargoJump_Anim`, `ftCo_CargoJump_IASA`, `ftCo_CargoJump_Phys`, and `ftCo_CargoJump_Coll`.
what_changed: Removed the `ftCo_CargoJump` assembly block from `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s`, including `ftCo_8009BB1C`, `ftCo_8009BB64`, `ftCo_CargoJump_Anim`, `ftCo_CargoJump_IASA`, `ftCo_CargoJump_Phys`, and `ftCo_CargoJump_Coll`.
Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CargoJump.c` implementing the matched cargo jump logic in C.
Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CargoJump.h` with declarations for the cargo jump functions and adjacent `ftCo_8009BC58` declaration.
Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CargoJump.c.o` to `obj_files.mk` before the remaining `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s.o`, preserving build order while splitting matched code out of the asm file.
Moved or exposed many fighter/common declarations through `src/melee/ft/ft_08A4.h`; the slice shows `src/melee/ft/ft_0877.h` losing 889 lines and `src/melee/ft/ft_08A4.h` gaining 885 lines.
Updated many fighter source files to include `ft/ft_08A4.h` where they still depended on symbols from the split header.
Updated cargo-related common files (`ftCo_CargoTurn.c`, `ftCo_CargoWait.c`, `ftCo_CargoWalk.c`) to include `ftCo_CargoJump.h` for direct cargo jump declarations.
Made a small control-flow cleanup in `ftDk_MS_345_0.c`, changing an `else { return false; }` after an early true return into a final `return false;`.
smart_moves: Kept the new C file narrow: `ftCo_CargoJump.c` only contains the matched cargo jump entry/helpers/callbacks instead of mixing unrelated ft_08A4 functions into the same translation unit.

Preserved action-callback structure by implementing empty `Anim`, `IASA`, `Phys`, and `Coll` callbacks with calls matching the removed assembly: `ftCo_8009BF3C`, `ftCo_800CB438`, and `ftCo_CargoFall_Coll`.

Used existing helper routines rather than duplicating behavior: `ftCommon_8007D5D4`, `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`, `ftAnim_SetAnimRate`, `ftCo_800CB110`, and `ftCo_8009C5A4` are composed to reproduce `ftCo_8009BB64`.
Documented a matching-specific artifact in `ftCo_8009BB64` with `/// @todo Unused stack.` and a `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` dummy stack allocation, making the non-obvious match constraint visible.
Added direct feature-local includes (`ftCo_CargoJump.h`) to cargo wait/walk/turn files instead of relying only on large umbrella headers.
decomp_lessons: When splitting matched functions out of a large assembly file, add the new C object before the remaining asm object in `obj_files.mk` so the original address/order context is maintained.

Header splits can cause widespread include churn. This PR appears to relocate many declarations from `ft_0877.h` to `ft_08A4.h`, requiring explicit `#include "ft/ft_08A4.h"` in many dependent fighter files.

For small action callback files, matching often depends on preserving apparently trivial callbacks: an empty animation callback, a single-call physics callback, and a collision callback forwarding to an existing fall collision routine.
Use `RETURN_IF(...)` macro style for IASA callbacks when the assembly is effectively a helper call followed by return; this can match short branch-and-return patterns cleanly.
Nonsemantic C refactors such as removing an unnecessary `else` after an early return can be part of include/header cleanup, but the evidence here does not show it as a central matching tactic.
If a function needs unused stack space only for matching, hide it behind `MUST_MATCH` and leave a clear TODO so future cleanup knows it is intentional.
naming_conventions: Common fighter cargo action files use the `ftCo_Cargo<Action>` pattern, e.g. `ftCo_CargoJump.c`, `ftCo_CargoJump.h`, `ftCo_CargoWait.c`, `ftCo_CargoWalk.c`, and `ftCo_CargoTurn.c`.
Action callback names follow the common Melee convention `ftCo_CargoJump_Anim`, `ftCo_CargoJump_IASA`, `ftCo_CargoJump_Phys`, and `ftCo_CargoJump_Coll`.
Unidentified or address-based common functions remain named with the `ftCo_8009....` convention, such as `ftCo_8009BB1C`, `ftCo_8009BB64`, and `ftCo_8009BC58`.
Header guards in this slice use address-derived names such as `GALE01_09BB1C` for `ftCo_CargoJump.h`.
Fighter object typedefs vary by context in the added code: entry helpers use `ftCo_GObj*`, while callbacks use `Fighter_GObj*` or `HSD_GObj*` according to existing callback signatures.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted the matched assembly functions from `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s` once the C implementation reported 100% matching.
Matched `ftCo_8009BB1C` as a boolean helper: call `ftCo_800CAE80(gobj)`, if true call `ftCo_8009B9C8(gobj, b)` and return true, otherwise return false.

Matched `ftCo_8009BB64` by reading `fp = gobj->user_data`, calling `ftCommon_8007D5D4(fp)`, changing motion state to `fp->x2CC->x4_motion_state + 7`, setting animation rate to zero, calling `ftCo_800CB110(gobj, 1, 1)`, then forcing the interacted fighter to `ftCo_MS_ShoulderedWait` via `ftCo_8009C5A4(fp->x1A58_interactedFighter, ftCo_MS_ShoulderedWait)`.

Used `NULL`, `0`, and `1` argument values in `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`, `ftAnim_SetAnimRate`, and `ftCo_800CB110` to reproduce the literal constants previously visible in assembly (`NULL`, `1.0`, and zero values).
Kept a `MUST_MATCH`-guarded unused 16-byte local array in `ftCo_8009BB64` to satisfy stack layout when required.
Matched tiny wrappers directly: `ftCo_CargoJump_Anim` is empty, `ftCo_CargoJump_Phys` calls `ftCo_800CB438`, and `ftCo_CargoJump_Coll` calls `ftCo_CargoFall_Coll`.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
The only human text excerpt is the generated match report showing 100.00% for the new `ftCo_CargoJump.c` file and its functions.
follow_up_queries: Inspect later PRs touching `ftCo_CargoJump.h` or `ftCo_8009BB64` to see whether the `MUST_MATCH` unused stack TODO was removed or better understood.
Search for follow-up header cleanup around `ft_0877.h` and `ft_08A4.h` to understand why many declarations were moved between those headers.
Compare neighboring cargo PRs for `ftCo_CargoKneebend`, `ftCo_CargoFall`, `ftCo_CargoWait`, `ftCo_CargoWalk`, and `ftCo_CargoTurn` to reuse callback and build-order patterns.
Look for later symbol renames of `ftCo_8009BB1C`, `ftCo_8009BB64`, and `ftCo_8009BC58` if their behavior became understood.
